title: Ulla Johnson Spring 2026 Collection is a Stunning Runway Masterpiece
coverAlt: Ulla Johnson Spring 2026 Collection is a Stunning Runway Masterpiece
excerpt: Ulla Johnson unveiled a 57-look collection for Spring/Summer 2026 inside the Cooper Hewitt Museum on 91st and 5th Avenue. Inspired by abstract expressionist painter Helen Frankenthaler, the collection
body:
Ulla Johnson unveiled a 57-look collection for Spring/Summer 2026 inside the Cooper Hewitt Museum on 91st and 5th Avenue — a venue steeped in personal meaning. As a native New Yorker, Johnson described the moment as full circle, returning to the very museum her mother once brought her to as a child.

The collection, inspired by abstract expressionist painter Helen Frankenthaler, unfolded like a living canvas. Textures came alive — fringes, ruffles, feathers, lace — creating a maximalist's dream. Silhouettes were punctuated with balloon sleeves, echoing the dramatic proportions seen at Aknvas and Christian Siriano this season.

Color moved beyond the familiar pastels of spring. Yes, there were peaches and lavenders, but the standout tones were sunset-inspired: mustard, rust, and indigo blues — a direct nod to Frankenthaler's painting Moontide.

Against her words, models drifted down the runway like brushstrokes across canvas — embodying beauty not as something proven, but simply as fact. This season, Johnson proposes a new kind of spring romanticism — one where texture takes precedence over florals, and beauty, far from obsolete, is reimagined as art in motion.
seoTitle: Ulla Johnson Spring 2026 Collection is a Stunning Runway Masterpiece
seoDesc: Ulla Johnson unveiled a 57-look collection for Spring/Summer 2026 inside the Cooper Hewitt Museum on 91st and 5th Avenue. Inspired by abstract expressionist pai

title: External Validation vs. Internal Validation: Choosing the Voice That Matters
coverAlt: External Validation vs. Internal Validation: Choosing the Voice That Matters
excerpt: A personal reflection on leaving the corporate world and breaking the cycle of seeking external validation. Explores why playing it safe keeps you stuck, and how internal validation rooted in faith an
body:
When I was working a 9–5, I thought I was doing the right thing. I thought following the 'stable' path would eventually lead me to security, recognition, and maybe even fulfillment. But truthfully? I didn't care so much about the promotions or recognition. What weighed on me more was not wanting to disappoint people. I wanted to fit in, to be seen as someone who was doing things 'the right way.'

But here's the part I never expected: no matter how much I tried to meet those expectations, I kept running into roadblocks. I couldn't climb the corporate ladder the way I thought I should. I found myself having to prove myself over and over again. And even when I gave my all, I still ended up being laid off or having to leave toxic workplaces.

That cycle taught me something important: living for external validation doesn't guarantee stability, success, or peace of mind. It just keeps you chasing after approval that may never come.

The Truth About Playing It Safe: I think a lot of us are seeking external validation, waiting for approval before we make a move. But playing it safe doesn't protect you the way you think it does. It just keeps you stuck, and the truth is, stuck is its own kind of failure.

At some point, you realize life isn't asking you to be safe, it's asking you to be real. Those desires aren't random — they're signals pointing you toward the life you're meant to build.

Why Internal Validation Lasts: Living in alignment with who you are, and creating a life that reflects your full potential, is the only validation that lasts. When I ground my worth in internal validation — rooted in faith, purpose, and truth — I can keep showing up even when no one else applauds.

Choosing Yourself Over Approval: Do I want to live for people's expectations, or do I want to live in alignment with God's calling? Internal validation may be quieter, but it's lasting. And that's the voice that matters most.
seoTitle: External Validation vs. Internal Validation: Choosing the Voice That M
seoDesc: A personal reflection on leaving the corporate world and breaking the cycle of seeking external validation. Explores why playing it safe keeps you stuck, and ho

title: Being Bold About My Beliefs as a Creator
coverAlt: Being Bold About My Beliefs as a Creator
excerpt: After years of staying quiet about her Christian faith in professional spaces, Nana Yaa commits to boldness as a content creator — trusting that God will bring the right brand partnerships to those wh
body:
For a long time, I was quiet about my faith and beliefs as a Christian. Anytime I mentioned it in a workplace, the attacks would come, and soon after, I'd find myself leaving. It almost felt like an attack on my livelihood.

But here's the thing: hiding who you are to fit in with people who don't truly value you will only attract the wrong people. Sometimes that hiding is just a cover, keeping you in a space where you don't even belong.

That's why, as I commit to my career as a content creator, I'm choosing courage. Whatever persecution comes, comes — but God will still get the glory and the honor as I walk in truth.

I trust that God will bring the right brands — the ones aligned with my values, and that value my creativity, my mind, and my perspective.

Other religions are free to express themselves openly, and Christians should be able to do the same. So I've decided not to hide my faith anymore. If I stay silent, I risk dimming the very light God gave me to shine.

At the end of the day, I believe the right doors will open, the right people will connect, and the right opportunities will come — not because I hid who I was, but because I stood firm in it.
seoTitle: Being Bold About My Beliefs as a Creator
seoDesc: After years of staying quiet about her Christian faith in professional spaces, Nana Yaa commits to boldness as a content creator — trusting that God will bring

title: Jo Malone's Ginger Biscuit is THE Gourmand Fragrance of the Season
coverAlt: Jo Malone's Ginger Biscuit is THE Gourmand Fragrance of the Season
excerpt: A review of Jo Malone's Ginger Biscuit fragrance — a cozy, spicy holiday gourmand with notes of ginger, roasted hazelnut, tonka bean, caramel, and woodsy tobacco. Limited edition.
body:
I've been on a run with my gourmand fragrances. I'm usually a woodsy vanilla girl but I started experimenting with olfactive families last year when I purchased the viral THAT GIRL fragrance from Oakcha. I was also gifted the Maison Margiela Replica Afternoon Delight. Both of these are delicious fragrances.

However, I had to take a second to pause when I learned about Jo Malone's Ginger Biscuit. The design is reminiscent of Rudolph's Red Nose which is so nostalgic.

A cozy, spicy but still delectable gourmand fragrance — it sounds like Christmas in a bottle. With top notes of Ginger, heart note of Roasted Hazelnut and base note of Tonka Bean. I love ginger, nutmeg and cinnamon especially during the holidays. All of these notes blended with caramel, tonka bean, vanilla and woodsy tobacco — this sounds like an irresistible dream.

I can't wait to get my hands on this. For sure, this is going on the wishlist. It's available for a limited time only.
seoTitle: Jo Malone's Ginger Biscuit is THE Gourmand Fragrance of the Season
seoDesc: A review of Jo Malone's Ginger Biscuit fragrance — a cozy, spicy holiday gourmand with notes of ginger, roasted hazelnut, tonka bean, caramel, and woodsy tobacc

title: Girl, Delulu is Not the Solulu — Having Faith Is.
coverAlt: Girl, Delulu is Not the Solulu — Having Faith Is.
excerpt: A pushback on the 'delulu is the solulu' trend — arguing that grounding your dreams in faith rather than delusional thinking is more empowering, purposeful, and lasting.
body:
If you've been anywhere on the internets, you'll see the common phrase that Delulu is the Solulu. Translation? Being delusional is the key to solving your problems. It's all fun and games, but honestly? I don't quite subscribe to this message.

I'm all for pursuing your wildest dreams, but why must we do it under the guise of being delusional? The definition of delusional is literally 'characterized by or holding false beliefs or judgments about external reality.' I think as women, we already get enough slack as it is. We shouldn't diminish our hopes and dreams into delusional thinking. They are worth taking seriously.

So no… delulu is not the solulu. Having faith is.

Faith is having a strong belief in the possibility that something might happen or that it will happen. That's the kind of positivity and light I want to shine over my dreams and goals. Not that I was so crazy to think it was real, but rather I believed in it so much that I worked towards making it a reality.

I'd rather be grounded in faith than lost in wishful thinking. Faith empowers us to move forward with intention, resilience, and purpose. So as you chase your dreams, let it be with conviction and trust in yourself — not because you're delusional, but because you know you're capable.
seoTitle: Girl, Delulu is Not the Solulu — Having Faith Is.
seoDesc: A pushback on the 'delulu is the solulu' trend — arguing that grounding your dreams in faith rather than delusional thinking is more empowering, purposeful, and

title: I Found the Best Cow Print Pieces for 2025—Here's How to Style It
coverAlt: I Found the Best Cow Print Pieces for 2025—Here's How to Style It
excerpt: A curated roundup of cow print fashion pieces for fall 2025, with styling tips for minimalists and maximalists alike.
body:
If a trend ever took over fall fashion, it would be the cow print trend. It's practically a neutral — with the earth tone colors of brown and black. It's surprisingly not that hard to style. You can wear a white tee under the jacket or a base color like brown or black to make the cow print stand out.

If you're on the maximalist side, experiment with complementary colors like pink, orange, or mustard. Or go wild with purples, greens, and yellows.

You can wear it subtly or as a bold statement — like with cow print boots, a skirt, or even a vest. Here are some of my favorite cow print pieces:

1. COW PRINT SAMBA LONG TONGUE SHOES (Adidas)
2. Satin Cow Print Midi Skirt
3. Sam Edelman Loraine Cow Print Calf Hair Bit Buckle Loafers
4. Cowgirl Glam Faux Fur Cow Print Coat (Fashion Nova)
5. STEVE MADDEN ANISE BROWN BAG
seoTitle: I Found the Best Cow Print Pieces for 2025—Here's How to Style It
seoDesc: A curated roundup of cow print fashion pieces for fall 2025, with styling tips for minimalists and maximalists alike.

And in my search for an alternative, I found one of the best — for a fraction of the price. Enter the Italian Leather Patchwork Crossbody from Quince.

Crafted from the finest Italian leather, this distinctive bag seamlessly blends bold design with exceptional quality. With just the right amount of space for your essentials, it's perfect for both an everyday look and a more refined evening vibe.

The Italian Leather Patchwork Crossbody is the epitome of sophisticated elegance and timeless allure — without the luxury price tag.

title: Why Being a Content Creator Is a Test of Faith
coverAlt: Why Being a Content Creator Is a Test of Faith
excerpt: Nana Yaa reflects on leaving her 9-5 and going all-in on content creation — relying entirely on faith, consistency, and God's provision.
body:
Being a content creator is a test of faith. Why? Because it's the complete opposite of what's expected of you. Working a 9-5 is expected of you. It's expected that you'll give 40 hours a week to a corporation that could get rid of you in a heartbeat.

Some people don't pursue their purpose because of this need for external validation — because they want to fit in. I was the same way for a while. I was too worried about having a stable job and steady income, doing things the 'right' way. But the right way still wasn't working. I realized that no job was truly stable, and that's when I decided to jump all in to my calling and not look back.

This has been one of my biggest tests of faith. I don't have anyone to depend on for a steady paycheck anymore. I can only depend on my efforts and on God's provision. I'm stretching my belief in myself and in God that this is going to work because, quite frankly, I have no other choice. It has to work.

When you cut the anchors of stability and safety nets, you can only count on yourself — and ultimately on God. And along the way, you face doubters and naysayers who don't understand the vision. Like Noah, mocked while building the Ark, you keep building what God placed inside of you — even when no one else sees it.

Because that's what faith really is: believing before you see, showing up before the results, and trusting that the seeds you plant today will grow into the future you prayed for tomorrow.
seoTitle: Why Being a Content Creator Is a Test of Faith
seoDesc: Nana Yaa reflects on leaving her 9-5 and going all-in on content creation — relying entirely on faith, consistency, and God's provision.

title: How to Pray for Guidance When You're Unsure About an Opportunity
coverAlt: How to Pray for Guidance When You're Unsure About an Opportunity
excerpt: A personal story about praying over a tempting but uncertain opportunity, watching it fall through, and finding peace in the outcome.
body:
Sometimes, answered prayers don't look the way you want them to. Sometimes they come in the form of a no… a closed door.

There was an opportunity that came through my email, which I thought would be a perfect fit. My funds were running low, and I thought this opportunity could hold me over. But I thought back to all the times I accepted opportunities that were seemingly good on the surface but eventually blew up in my face.

So I prayed: 'God, if you want this for me, it has to be completely right all the way through to the end of it. And if it's a distraction and it's not meant for me, please take it away.'

It turns out that the opportunity fell through. It was actually a carrot-on-a-stick kind of opportunity, not even solidified. At first, I was frustrated and annoyed.

But then I was reminded of my prayer, and I was left with a peaceful assurance. It wouldn't have been the right fit, and God knew that. This was probably just a sneak peek of the culture I'd be dealing with, and it probably wouldn't have ended well.

All that glitters isn't gold, and I didn't need to learn that lesson twice.
seoTitle: How to Pray for Guidance When You're Unsure About an Opportunity
seoDesc: A personal story about praying over a tempting but uncertain opportunity, watching it fall through, and finding peace in the outcome.

title: 6 Gorgeous Work Bags That Will Make You Stand Out at the Office
coverAlt: 6 Gorgeous Work Bags That Will Make You Stand Out at the Office
excerpt: A guide to elevated work bags — the structured tote, mini work satchel, statement bag, and more — with styling tips for each.
body:
Your work bag is more than just a catch-all for your laptop and notebooks — it's an extension of your personal style. The right bag doesn't just carry your essentials; it elevates your entire look, signaling polish, professionalism, and personality.

1. The Structured Tote — Classic, timeless, and roomy enough for your laptop. Look for clean lines, quality leather, and neutral tones like black, tan, or navy. Invest in one with a zip closure to keep everything secure during commutes.

2. The Mini Work Satchel — If you don't need to carry much besides your tablet, planner, and wallet, a smaller structured satchel is a chic option. Perfect for hybrid workers. Pair with dresses, midi skirts, or smart casual outfits.

3. The Statement Bag — A bold bag in a rich color (burgundy, forest green, or deep plum) or textured finish (croc-embossed) makes a subtle but powerful statement. Keep the rest of your outfit neutral and let the bag do the talking.

Your work bag is an investment — one you use daily and that speaks volumes about your style.
seoTitle: 6 Gorgeous Work Bags That Will Make You Stand Out at the Office
seoDesc: A guide to elevated work bags — the structured tote, mini work satchel, statement bag, and more — with styling tips for each.

title: How to Create a Personal Style You Love—Effortlessly
coverAlt: How to Create a Personal Style You Love—Effortlessly
excerpt: An introduction to Nana Yaa's ebook covering style DNA discovery, wardrobe building, smarter shopping, and confidence-boosting styling tricks.
body:
Ever stand in front of your closet and think, 'I have nothing to wear' even though it's full of clothes? You're not alone. Most of us struggle with our wardrobes — not because we lack clothes, but because we haven't discovered our personal style yet.

Do you ever feel like: you have a closet full of clothes but nothing feels right? You spend way too much time getting dressed in the morning? You buy trendy pieces that never end up working with anything else? If this sounds familiar, it's not your fault. Most people never learned how to define their style or build a wardrobe that actually works for their life.

Your personal style is more than just clothes — it's how you express yourself. When you know your style, getting dressed becomes effortless. You feel confident, put-together, and authentically you.

Introducing 'How to Create a Personal Style You Love' — a step-by-step guide to discovering your authentic style and building a wardrobe that makes every day easier and more stylish. Inside, you'll learn how to: discover your unique style DNA, build a cohesive wardrobe that mixes and matches effortlessly, shop smarter to avoid impulse buys, and master styling tricks that instantly elevate your look.
seoTitle: How to Create a Personal Style You Love—Effortlessly
seoDesc: An introduction to Nana Yaa's ebook covering style DNA discovery, wardrobe building, smarter shopping, and confidence-boosting styling tricks.

title: The 3-3-3 Rule for Clothing: Simplify Your Wardrobe and Elevate Your Style
coverAlt: The 3-3-3 Rule for Clothing: Simplify Your Wardrobe and Elevate Your Style
excerpt: A breakdown of the 3-3-3 wardrobe formula: 3 tops, 3 bottoms, 3 pairs of shoes — creating dozens of outfits from just 9 intentional pieces.
body:
Have you ever opened your closet, felt overwhelmed, and thought 'I have nothing to wear'? The truth is, most of us have too many clothes — but not enough intentional pieces that actually work together. That's where the 3-3-3 Rule for Clothing comes in.

What Is the 3-3-3 Rule? A simple wardrobe formula: 3 tops, 3 bottoms, 3 pairs of shoes. With just 9 carefully chosen pieces, you can mix and match to create dozens of outfits.

Why It Works: Less Overwhelm (fewer options = easier decisions), More Versatility (each piece chosen intentionally), Polished Style (cohesive mini-wardrobe), Travel-Friendly (perfect for packing light).

How to Build It: Choose Neutral Basics, Pick Mix-and-Match Bottoms, Add Shoes That Work Double Duty, Stick to a Color Palette.

Example: Tops — white button-up, black t-shirt, beige sweater. Bottoms — blue jeans, black trousers, midi skirt. Shoes — white sneakers, black ankle boots, nude flats.

The 3-3-3 Rule proves that you don't need an overflowing closet to have amazing style. With a handful of versatile, well-chosen pieces, you can look polished and confident every day.
seoTitle: The 3-3-3 Rule for Clothing: Simplify Your Wardrobe and Elevate Your S
seoDesc: A breakdown of the 3-3-3 wardrobe formula: 3 tops, 3 bottoms, 3 pairs of shoes — creating dozens of outfits from just 9 intentional pieces.

title: How Do You Develop Your Personal Style?
coverAlt: How Do You Develop Your Personal Style?
excerpt: A step-by-step guide to developing personal style: auditing your wardrobe, identifying style icons, defining style goals, and evolving over time.
body:
Personal style isn't something you wake up with one day — it's something you develop over time. Think of it like a muscle: the more you practice and refine it, the stronger it gets.

1. Start With What You Already Have — Audit your current wardrobe. Which pieces do you love and wear often? Which ones sit untouched?

2. Identify Your Style Icons — Think of people whose outfits inspire you. Break down what you like: Is it their color palette? Silhouettes? Accessories?

3. Define Your Style Goals — What do I want my style to say about me? Do you want to look polished and professional, laid-back and effortless, or bold and fashion-forward?

4. Experiment With Intention — Try new pieces but be strategic: add one new element at a time. Pay attention to how it makes you feel.

5. Build a Core Wardrobe — Invest in quality basics that fit your lifestyle — like a tailored blazer, well-fitting denim, or the perfect pair of shoes.

6. Evolve With Time — Your personal style isn't static. Keep refining — revisiting your wardrobe regularly.

7. Pay Attention to Fit and Details — Even the best outfit ideas fall flat if the fit is off. Tailoring, accessories, and grooming elevate a look from 'okay' to 'effortlessly stylish.'
seoTitle: How Do You Develop Your Personal Style?
seoDesc: A step-by-step guide to developing personal style: auditing your wardrobe, identifying style icons, defining style goals, and evolving over time.

title: How to Find Your Personal Style (A Step by Step Guide)
coverAlt: How to Find Your Personal Style (A Step by Step Guide)
excerpt: A practical guide to finding your personal style through visual inspiration, lifestyle consideration, defining style words, and building a cohesive wardrobe.
body:
If you've ever stood in front of your closet and thought 'I have nothing to wear' — you're not alone. The real issue usually isn't the number of clothes you own. It's clarity. You haven't defined your personal style yet.

1. Look for Style Inspiration — Gather visuals you're drawn to: Pinterest, Instagram, magazines, TV characters. Look for patterns in what you save.

2. Pay Attention to What You Actually Wear — Your real-life style shows up in what you reach for daily. Your personal style is usually hiding in plain sight.

3. Consider Your Lifestyle — Style should serve your life. If you're always on the go, you'll need practical yet chic staples. A style you can't actually live in won't stick.

4. Define Your Style Words — Choose 3–5 words that describe the style you want (e.g. 'minimal, timeless, feminine'). These act like a filter when shopping.

5. Experiment and Refine — Don't be afraid to try new silhouettes or colors. Pay attention to how they make you feel.

6. Build a Cohesive Wardrobe — Curate pieces that work together. A wardrobe full of intentional items makes outfit-building effortless.
seoTitle: How to Find Your Personal Style (A Step by Step Guide)
seoDesc: A practical guide to finding your personal style through visual inspiration, lifestyle consideration, defining style words, and building a cohesive wardrobe.

title: What is Personal Style? (and how to find yours)
coverAlt: What is Personal Style? (and how to find yours)
excerpt: An explainer on personal style — what it is, why it matters, how it differs from fashion trends, and four steps to start finding yours.
body:
We've all had those mornings — standing in front of a closet full of clothes and still feeling like we have nothing to wear. It's rarely about how many pieces we own. It's about whether those pieces reflect our personal style.

At its core, personal style is the way you consistently express yourself through clothing. It's less about following every trend and more about building a wardrobe that feels authentic, cohesive, and effortless.

Why Personal Style Matters: Clarity & Confidence (your closet stops being overwhelming), Consistency (outfits naturally go together), Self-Expression (style communicates who you are before you even say a word).

Personal Style vs. Fashion Trends: Fashion trends come and go, but your signature style is timeless. Think of trends as seasoning you sprinkle in — while your personal style is the foundation of your wardrobe.

How to Find Your Personal Style: Notice What You're Drawn To, Evaluate Your Lifestyle Needs, Experiment Intentionally, Invest in Staples.
seoTitle: What is Personal Style? (and how to find yours)
seoDesc: An explainer on personal style — what it is, why it matters, how it differs from fashion trends, and four steps to start finding yours.

title: 5 Exciting Spring Runway Trends You'll Be Seeing Everywhere
coverAlt: 5 Exciting Spring Runway Trends You'll Be Seeing Everywhere
excerpt: Spring 2026 runway roundup: chartreuse, champagne tones, statement balloon sleeves, rich textures, and the return of animal prints and polka dots.
body:
Forget florals — spring 2026 is rewriting the fashion playbook. Designers sent down runways filled with bold colors, dramatic shapes, and playful details that prove fashion is all about joy and self-expression.

1. Chartreuse Is The Color of the Season — Sergio Hudson and Ulla Johnson showed just how powerful this vibrant, high-energy shade can be. Bold, risky, and guaranteed to turn heads.

2. Champagne Tones — Butter yellow's older, more refined sister. Luxurious, understated, but still fresh — works for everything from fluid dresses to tailored suiting.

3. Statement Sleeves — Christian Siriano and Prabal Gurung leaned into exaggerated balloon and puff sleeves, giving silhouettes that feel both romantic and dramatic.

4. The Texture Party — Fringes, sequins, feathers, lace, ruffles. Fabrics are doing the heavy lifting this season.

5. Animal Prints & Polka Dots — Leopard and zebra for spring, alongside polka dots at PatBo and Christian Siriano.

Spring 2026 is a maximalist's dream. This season is all about having fun with fashion, taking risks, and letting your clothes bring a little joy into your day.
seoTitle: 5 Exciting Spring Runway Trends You'll Be Seeing Everywhere
seoDesc: Spring 2026 runway roundup: chartreuse, champagne tones, statement balloon sleeves, rich textures, and the return of animal prints and polka dots.

title: Inside Sergio Hudson's Bold and Beautiful 2026 Show: Chartreuse, Puff Sleeves, and African-Inspired Prints
coverAlt: Inside Sergio Hudson's Bold and Beautiful 2026 Show: Chartreuse, Puff Sleeves, and African-Inspired Prints
excerpt: A recap of Sergio Hudson's SS26 collection featuring chartreuse, vibrant violets, puff sleeves, and digitized mudcloth tribal prints bridging heritage and modernity.
body:
Sergio Hudson's Spring/Summer 2026 collection was nothing short of a celebration. Known for his empowering, tailored, and colorful aesthetic, Hudson doubled down on what makes his work so distinct — boldness with a feminine edge.

Chartreuse took center stage, while vibrant violets and other saturated hues rounded out the palette. Puff sleeves and oversized accessories brought drama.

One of the collection's standout elements was Hudson's reinterpretation of African inspiration. Mudcloth tribal patterns were digitized and transformed into pop-art prints, creating a powerful bridge between heritage and modernity.

Beyond the clothes, Hudson's message was clear: fashion should bring joy. He wants women to feel the delight of getting dressed — not simply fill their closets with pieces that fade into the background.

The front row included notable guests like Monet McMichael, Jackie Aina, Tenicka Boyd, Jenee Naylor, Mary J. Blige, and June Ambrose. Hudson's SS26 show wasn't just a runway — it was a joyful reminder that color, print, and unapologetic femininity never go out of style.
seoTitle: Inside Sergio Hudson's Bold and Beautiful 2026 Show: Chartreuse, Puff
seoDesc: A recap of Sergio Hudson's SS26 collection featuring chartreuse, vibrant violets, puff sleeves, and digitized mudcloth tribal prints bridging heritage and moder

title: 2 Affordable Looks You Could Wear to the US Open
coverAlt: 2 Affordable Looks You Could Wear to the US Open
excerpt: Two outfit ideas for the US Open: a playful sporty look and a prim and polished look with the Meshki Mackenzie Sleeveless Pleated Mini Dress.
body:
I wouldn't call myself a tennis expert, but I'll never forget watching Serena Williams play her last match at the U.S. Open. The energy, the history, the way the whole stadium seemed to hold its breath — it was unforgettable. Since then, I've always had a soft spot for the Open. It's not just a sporting event, it's a cultural one — where fashion, celebrity, and sport all collide.

Look 1: Playful & Sporty — A tennis-inspired skirt or dress in bright colors or stripes. Pair with fun sneakers and a visor. Featured: ALINA PLEATED KNIT FIT AND FLARE MINI DRESS IN NAVY.

Look 2: Prim & Polished — The Meshki Mackenzie Sleeveless Pleated Mini Dress is the perfect mix of elegance and contemporary design. The drop-waist detail and pleated skirt give a subtle nod to the classic tennis skirt. Ideal for staying comfortable while looking effortlessly put-together — and easily transitions into an evening look.
seoTitle: 2 Affordable Looks You Could Wear to the US Open

title: Fashion Week Isn't for the Public Anymore — But the Public Is Still Watching
coverAlt: Fashion Week Isn't for the Public Anymore — But the Public Is Still Watching
excerpt: An editorial on the return of invite-only culture at NYFW, the end of Fashion Night Out, and how influencers have become the public's only real access point to the runway.
body:
Fashion Week used to be where the future of fashion was revealed. But as the front rows fill with celebrities, private clients, and handpicked press, the public has been pushed further from the experience. Livestreams are limited. Media access is shrinking. For most people, the only glimpse they get of the runway is from influencers.

The Return of Invite-Only Culture: Major brands are limiting livestreams — with over 60 brands partnering with CFDA, only one-third chose to livestream in partnership with Rockefeller Center.

Fashion's Night Out: Launched in 2009 by Vogue, CFDA, and the NYC government, FNO created inclusion and accessibility. It was halted in 2013 due to a lack of ROI and retail fatigue.

Influencers: The Public's New Front Row: Influencers have become the bridge between exclusive fashion shows and the public's desire for access, sharing real-time moments that official channels no longer provide.

The Rise of 'Influencer Fashion Week': Most people don't watch the actual shows — they experience Fashion Week through influencers. Fashion Week might not want the public anymore — but the public is still watching, through someone else's camera roll.
seoTitle: Fashion Week Isn't for the Public Anymore — But the Public Is Still Wa
seoDesc: An editorial on the return of invite-only culture at NYFW, the end of Fashion Night Out, and how influencers have become the public's only real access point to

title: Social Media, Personal Style, and the Allure of Fashion Week: The Antidote We Need?
coverAlt: Social Media, Personal Style, and the Allure of Fashion Week: The Antidote We Need?
excerpt: A reflection on how social media is homogenizing personal style, and why Fashion Week feels like a rare antidote to algorithmic dressing.
body:
Social media has made fashion more accessible than ever, but it's also making us all look the same. Mood boards, Pinterest outfits, and influencer-approved formulas make it easy to copy a look, but somewhere along the way, we've lost our personal style. Fashion is starting to feel like a performance rather than something fun, something that's ours.

That's why I love New York Fashion Week.

Why Fashion Week Feels Different: In the crisp early-fall air, the city feels alive in a way social media can't replicate. The streets become runways, full of people taking risks and experimenting. Maximalists walk next to minimalists, vintage lovers side by side with avant-garde dreamers, and no two outfits look the same.

A Playground for Individuality: Fashion Week is this rare moment when individuality dominates the feed and the streets. People are willing to play with layers, clash colors, mix textures, and express who they are — without worrying about whether it's trending.

Dressing for Yourself, Not the Algorithm: My hope is that this energy seeps into our everyday lives. That we stop dressing for the algorithm and start dressing for ourselves. Fashion Week is more than shows and street style — it's a reminder that individuality still matters. That personal style isn't dead.
seoTitle: Social Media, Personal Style, and the Allure of Fashion Week: The Anti
seoDesc: A reflection on how social media is homogenizing personal style, and why Fashion Week feels like a rare antidote to algorithmic dressing.

title: 4 Times Love Island's Olandria Carthen Slayed Fashion Week

excerpt: Four standout NYFW looks from Love Island US star Olandria Carthen across LaPointe, Alexander Wang, Valentino Beauty, and Off-White.
body:
A hot new bombshell has entered the NYFW streets, and Olandria has been serving look after look this season.

1. LaPointe Monochrome Glam — Leopard-print calf hair coat with matching high-waisted mini shorts, layered over a sheer black bodysuit. Showstopper: a bejewelled basketball purse. Grounded with Femme LA G63 Slingbacks in Patent Black.

2. Power Moves at Alexander Wang — Oversized blazer cinched by a studded belt, finished with a spiked mini purse. Downtown edge meets bold femininity.

3. Studio 54 Barbie with Valentino Beauty — At the 'Born in Roma' Studio 54 launch, full Bama Barbie energy in a Harbison Studio ensemble.

4. Sparkle & Sleek at Off-White — A deep-plunge, long-sleeved bodysuit paired with a rhinestone skirt. Simple in palette but dazzling in effect.

title: Why You Should Trust the Timing of Your Life
coverAlt: Why You Should Trust the Timing of Your Life
excerpt: After being laid off and narrowly avoiding a car accident, Nana Yaa reflects on divine timing — how disruptions and closed doors are forms of protection and alignment.
body:
I was recently laid off, but the funny thing about it is I knew in my spirit I had to leave. I just wasn't sure how to go about it, so God did it for me. And when it happened, I felt so relieved — I couldn't even hide my smile. Instead of fear or anxiety, I felt peace.

Not long after that, my mom and I almost had an accident when the steering wheel in her car suddenly stopped working. The good part is that it happened close to home, right after we had finished running errands. If we hadn't done those errands that day, the steering wheel probably would've stopped working on the highway — so much more dangerous.

That moment gave me so much gratitude. Delays, disruptions, even layoffs — they're not accidents. They're protection. They're alignment. They're God stepping in when we don't know how to move ourselves.

So if you're in a season of change or uncertainty right now, remember: the timing of your life isn't random — it's ordered. Every shift, every pause, every closed door is working for your good.

Reflection: Where have I been resisting change, even though my spirit knew it was time to move? What delays or disruptions in my life might actually be protection or guidance?

'And we know that all things work together for the good of those who love him and are called according to His purpose.' — Romans 8:28
seoTitle: Why You Should Trust the Timing of Your Life
seoDesc: After being laid off and narrowly avoiding a car accident, Nana Yaa reflects on divine timing — how disruptions and closed doors are forms of protection and ali

title: The Only Boots Worth Making Closet Space For This Season
coverAlt: The Only Boots Worth Making Closet Space For This Season
excerpt: A feature on the Sam Edelman Sylvia Knee High Boots — a suede, almond-toe, block-heel staple perfect for fall.
body:
The Sylvia Knee High Boots from Sam Edelman are the kind of timeless staple every fall wardrobe needs. Sleek and sophisticated, they're designed with a classic knee-high silhouette that elongates the leg and pairs effortlessly with everything from dresses to denim.

Crafted in suede with a refined almond toe and block heel, they strike the perfect balance between style and comfort — making them wearable for both everyday and elevated occasions.

What I love most is their versatility: dress them up with a blazer dress for a chic night out, or keep it cozy with an oversized sweater and straight leg jeans. Either way, these boots instantly pull your outfit together and give you that effortless, polished finish.

title: The Affordable Alternative to the Saint Laurent Lee Slingback Pumps
coverAlt: The Affordable Alternative to the Saint Laurent Lee Slingback Pumps
excerpt: The YSL Lee Slingback Pumps reimagined at a fraction of the cost — an Amazon dupe that delivers the same chic slingback silhouette.
body:
The YSL Lee Slingback Pumps are the definition of modern sophistication. Crafted in smooth calfskin leather, these heels feature a sharp square-pointed toe, a sleek square-cut vamp, and an elasticized slingback strap for a secure fit. The standout detail: a gold-plated geometric stiletto heel in polished brass that turns every step into a statement. Equal parts architectural and timeless.

The YSL Lee Slingback Pumps are a work of art but with a luxury price tag, they're not in everyone's budget.

That's why I had to share this affordable alternative I found on Amazon. It gives you the same chic slingback silhouette and polished vibe for a fraction of the price. Whether you're building a capsule wardrobe or just want the look for less, this dupe lets you channel Saint Laurent sophistication without breaking the bank.

title: The Cinched Waist Blazer Dress You Need for Fall
coverAlt: The Cinched Waist Blazer Dress You Need for Fall
excerpt: A fall outfit guide built around a cream blazer dress with burgundy heels and gold accessories, with three styling variants: day, evening, and cooler weather.
body:
Fall fashion is all about cozy textures, rich tones, and effortlessly chic layers. One of my go-to looks this season is a tailored blazer dress paired with burgundy heels and accessories — a look that's polished, feminine, and versatile enough to take you from day to night.

Core Pieces: Blazer Dress (structured and tailored, can be dressed up or down), Burgundy Heels (deep jewel tones are perfect for fall), Accessories (minimal gold jewelry and a structured bag).

Styling Tips:
- Day Look: Add sheer tights and pointed-toe flats or loafers, paired with an oversized tote.
- Evening Look: Swap flats for your burgundy heels, add bold lipstick, and layer on a few gold hoops.
- Cooler Weather Look: Layer with black tights, knee-high boots, and a belted coat.

Why This Outfit Works: Combines classic tailoring with a pop of fall color. Effortless transition from day to night. Easy to restyle with seasonal accessories.
seoTitle: The Cinched Waist Blazer Dress You Need for Fall
seoDesc: A fall outfit guide built around a cream blazer dress with burgundy heels and gold accessories, with three styling variants: day, evening, and cooler weather.

title: Discover Miss Dior Essence: A Fragrance for the Modern Woman
coverAlt: Discover Miss Dior Essence: A Fragrance for the Modern Woman
excerpt: A review of Miss Dior Essence by Francis Kurkdjian — a modern chypre with blackberry, elderflower, jasmine sambac, and woody oak for the confident, daring modern woman.
body:
Imagined by Francis Kurkdjian, Dior's Perfume Creative Director, Miss Dior Essence is a powerful symbol of magnetic femininity. With its high concentration and radiant trail, the fragrance saturates the senses in color, weaving together gourmand and sensual accords.

Unexpected yet captivating, it opens with a luscious blackberry and elderflower accord that lifts a sumptuous jasmine bouquet. At its core, jasmine sambac blooms with sensual intensity, while a grounding base of rich, woody oak brings depth and sophistication.

This modern chypre is playful yet commanding — a fragrance that feels like a bold brushstroke across a floral canvas. With its jammy, floral, and woody layers, Miss Dior Essence speaks to a new generation of women: confident, daring, and unapologetically living life on their own terms.
seoTitle: Discover Miss Dior Essence: A Fragrance for the Modern Woman
seoDesc: A review of Miss Dior Essence by Francis Kurkdjian — a modern chypre with blackberry, elderflower, jasmine sambac, and woody oak for the confident, daring moder

title: My Miss Circle Look at Sip N' Slay NYC 2025
coverAlt: My Miss Circle Look at Sip N' Slay NYC 2025
excerpt: A recap of the EveryStylishGirl Sip N' Slay event in NYC — Nana Yaa wore the Miss Circle Raelyn Dress in black-and-white, standing out through contrast in a room full of vivid color.
body:
Today I attended the EveryStylishGirl's Sip N' Slay event in New York City, and it was exactly the kind of stylish, inspiring, and empowering gathering that makes this city so special. The room was filled with ambitious, fashion-forward women — all sipping, networking, and slaying in their own unique way.

The room came alive with a spectrum of bold, bright colors — hot pinks, lime greens, electric blues. But I decided to take a completely different approach. Instead of chasing vibrancy, I leaned into a monochromatic palette — polished, elegant, and undeniably sophisticated.

For the occasion, I wore the Raelyn Dress by Miss Circle in its classic black-and-white design. The crisp white collar paired with the sleek black bodycon silhouette gave me an edge that was subtle, but impossible to ignore. While many leaned into boldness through color, I created boldness through contrast.

The Raelyn Dress blends business casual sophistication with vintage-inspired charm. The high-waist design and slim fit silhouette enhanced my curves, while the bodycon cut kept the look sleek and polished. A subtle back slit gave effortless movement.

The event featured panels on 'The Creator Code: Influence, Income & Integrity' and 'Monetize with Meaning.' Walking away, I felt inspired and recharged. Fashion isn't just about what you wear — it's about how you carry yourself and how a dress can help you step into the room with a little extra sparkle.
seoTitle: My Miss Circle Look at Sip N' Slay NYC 2025
seoDesc: A recap of the EveryStylishGirl Sip N' Slay event in NYC — Nana Yaa wore the Miss Circle Raelyn Dress in black-and-white, standing out through contrast in a roo
